title: GOP operatives boost Cornel West’s efforts to get on Wisconsin ballot
shortDescription: Canvassers for a Republican consulting firm were among those who turned in petitions to get liberal academic Cornel West on the ballot in Wisconsin.

title: Omar wins, Wisconsin Senate fight is set: 3 takeaways from Tuesday's primaries
shortDescription: Ilhan Omar's victory caps off a series of primaries facing the Squad, while Eric Hovde and Tammy Baldwin will face off in one of the most competitive Senate races in the country.

title: What are voters expecting out of a Trump, Harris debate?
shortDescription: Young voters are weighing in on their expectations for the first debate between Donald Trump and Kamala Harris

title: Trump's campaign office burglarized, authorities search for suspect
shortDescription: The Loudoun County Sheriff’s Office is searching for a man they suspect burglarized a campaign office for former president Donald Trump.

title: 'Appalled': Veterans' letter slams Trump attacks on Walz's service
shortDescription: More than 1,000 veterans and family members signed a letter saying they are "appalled" by the Trump campaign's attack's on Tim Walz's service.

title: Clip of Trump pausing during rally misrepresented online | Fact check
shortDescription: Trump didn't freeze, he paused while an audience member was treated by a medic.

title: Isaac Hayes' family sues Trump campaign, Celine Dion speaks out
shortDescription: The family of Isaac Hayes is suing the Trump campaign and Celine Dion is issuing a statement over the use of their music at rallies.

title: White House launches crackdown on customer service 'doom loops'
shortDescription: The White House announced Monday a new initiative to crack down on practices such as chatbots, 'doom loops' and excessive paperwork.

title: Election-denying official convicted of felonies tied to 2020 theories
shortDescription: Tina Peters, a former Mesa County, Colorado, clerk accused of tampering with voting machines was found guilty of four felony charges.

title: Trump says he will return to Butler, Pennsylvania, in October
shortDescription: Former President Donald Trump said he plans to return this fall to Butler, Pennsylvania, where he was shot in a July rally.

title: Trump praises Harris' Time Magazine cover image
shortDescription: Kamala Harris now graces the cover of Time Magazine’s latest issue following her campaign appearance in Pennsylvania alongside her running mate, Tim Walz.

title: Biden targets surgery with $150 million for 'Cancer Moonshot'
shortDescription: New federal funding will aim to improve surgery for solid tumors.

title: FBI probes Trump hack, Harris campaign also targeted | The Excerpt
shortDescription: Plus: New polling data spells trouble for former president Donald Trump. | Why millions of bird flu vaccines are bing prepped.

title: Trump says he'll return to Butler where he was shot in the ear
shortDescription: In an audio interview with Elon Musk on X, former president Donald Trump said he will return to Butler, where he was shot in the ear.

title: Tech issues plague Trump, Musk interview
shortDescription: X owner Elon Musk said that the tech issues were caused by a cyber attack, but that has yet to be confirmed.

title: Stream for Elon Musk-Donald Trump chat crashes
shortDescription: The live-streamed interview between Elon Musk and Donald Trump crashed on X.

title: Ilhan Omar fights for reelection after 'the Squad' sees major losses
shortDescription: Rep. Ilhan Omar, a member of the progressive 'Squad,' faces a fight for her political life Tuesday in a closely watched primary showdown.

title: Ilhan Omar competes, Wisconsin Senate showdown looms: Primaries to watch
shortDescription: Voters will decide the outcome of Senate and House primaries in four states Tuesday, Aug. 13.

title: Harris within 'striking distance' of Trump in Florida
shortDescription: A new USA TODAY/Suffolk University survey shows Trump leading Harris, but within the margin of error

title: Robert F. Kennedy Jr. blocked from New York presidential ballot
shortDescription: A New York judge ruled Robert F. Kennedy Jr. falsely claimed a New York address as his residence and ordered that he not appear on the ballot.
